revision.text:
{{Japanese name|Katsuragi}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 葛城氏 |image = |image size = 140px |image caption = |home province = |parent house =[[File:Imperial Seal of Japan.svg|15px]] [[Imperial House of Japan]] |founder =Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko
(葛城襲津彦) |founding year =4th century |cadet branches = }} The {{nihongo|'''Katsuragi clan'''|葛城氏|Katsuragi-uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group (''[[Uji (clan)|uji]]'') during the [[Kofun period]]. [[Edmund Papinot|Papinot, Jacques Edmond Joseph]]. (1906). ''Dictionnaire d’histoire et de géographie du Japon''; Papinot, (2003). [http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5. ==Origin== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城襲津彦), a great-grandson of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. ==Family Tree== *[[Emperor Kōgen]] (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of [[Emperor Yūryaku]] *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******[[Princess Iwa|Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto]] (磐之媛命), empress of [[Emperor Nintoku]] ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of [[Emperor Richū]] *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of [[Emperor Richū]] ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼) ==See Also== *[[Kofun period]] *[[Soga clan]] *[[Nakatomi clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] ==References==

revision.text:
{{Japanese name|Katsuragi}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 葛城氏 |image = |image size = 140px |image caption = |home province = |parent house =[[File:Imperial Seal of Japan.svg|15px]] [[Imperial House of Japan]] |founder =Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko
(葛城襲津彦) |founding year =4th century |cadet branches = }} The {{nihongo|'''Katsuragi clan'''|葛城氏|Katsuragi-uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group (''[[Uji (clan)|uji]]'') during the [[Kofun period]]. [[Edmund Papinot|Papinot, Jacques Edmond Joseph]]. (1906). ''Dictionnaire d’histoire et de géographie du Japon''; Papinot, (2003). [http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5. ==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城襲津彦), a great-grandson of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the [[Ōtomo clan]] temporarily took its place. When [[Emperor Buretsu]] died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended [[Emperor Keitai]], a very distant imperial relative who resided in [[Koshi Province]], as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the [[Asuka period]]. ==Family Tree== *[[Emperor Kōgen]] (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of [[Emperor Yūryaku]] *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******[[Princess Iwa|Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto]] (磐之媛命), empress of [[Emperor Nintoku]] ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of [[Emperor Richū]] *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of [[Emperor Richū]] ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼) ==See Also== *[[Kofun period]] *[[Soga clan]] *[[Nakatomi clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] ==References==

{{Japanese name|Katsuragi}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 葛城氏 |image = |image size = 140px |image caption = |home province = |parent house =[[File:Imperial Seal of Japan.svg|15px]] [[Imperial House of Japan]] |founder =Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko
(葛城襲津彦) |founding year =4th century |cadet branches = }} The {{nihongo|'''Katsuragi clan'''|葛城氏|Katsuragi-uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group (''[[Uji (clan)|uji]]'') during the [[Kofun period]]. [[Edmund Papinot|Papinot, Jacques Edmond Joseph]]. (1906). ''Dictionnaire d’histoire et de géographie du Japon''; Papinot, (2003). [http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5. ==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城襲津彦), a great-grandson of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the [[Ōtomo clan]] temporarily took its place. When [[Emperor Buretsu]] died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended [[Emperor Keitai]], a very distant imperial relative who resided in [[Koshi Province]], as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the [[Asuka period]].Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. ISBN 0-521-22352-0. ==Family Tree== *[[Emperor Kōgen]] (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of [[Emperor Yūryaku]] *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******[[Princess Iwa|Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto]] (磐之媛命), empress of [[Emperor Nintoku]] ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of [[Emperor Richū]] *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of [[Emperor Richū]] ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼) ==See Also== *[[Kofun period]] *[[Soga clan]] *[[Nakatomi clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] ==References==

revision.text:
{{refimprove|date=December 2017}} {{family name hatnote|Katsuragi|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 葛城氏 |image = |image size = 140px |image caption = |home province = |parent house =[[File:Imperial Seal of Japan.svg|15px]] [[Imperial House of Japan]] |founder =Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko
(葛城襲津彦) |founding year =4th century |cadet branches = }} The {{nihongo|'''Katsuragi clan'''|葛城氏|Katsuragi-uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group (''[[Uji (clan)|uji]]'') during the [[Kofun period]]. [[Edmund Papinot|Papinot, Jacques Edmond Joseph]]. (1906). ''Dictionnaire d’histoire et de géographie du Japon''; Papinot, (2003). [http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5. ==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城襲津彦), a great-grandson of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the [[Ōtomo clan]] temporarily took its place. When [[Emperor Buretsu]] died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended [[Emperor Keitai]], a very distant imperial relative who resided in [[Koshi Province]], as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the [[Asuka period]].Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}. ==Family Tree== *[[Emperor Kōgen]] (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of [[Emperor Yūryaku]] *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******[[Princess Iwa|Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto]] (磐之媛命), empress of [[Emperor Nintoku]] ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of [[Emperor Richū]] *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of [[Emperor Richū]] ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼) ==See Also== *[[Kofun period]] *[[Soga clan]] *[[Nakatomi clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] ==References== [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese clans]]

revision.comment: [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Capital letters|decapitalise]], replaced: ==See Also== → ==See also==

Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}. ==Family Tree== *[[Emperor Kōgen]] (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of [[Emperor Yūryaku]] *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******[[Princess Iwa|Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto]] (磐之媛命), empress of [[Emperor Nintoku]] ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of [[Emperor Richū]] *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of [[Emperor Richū]] ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼) ==See also== *[[Kofun period]] *[[Soga clan]] *[[Nakatomi clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] ==References== [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese clans]]

{{refimprove|date=December 2017}} {{family name hatnote|Katsuragi|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 葛城氏 |image = |image size = 140px |image caption = |home province = |parent house =[[File:Imperial Seal of Japan.svg|15px]] [[Imperial House of Japan]] |founder =Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko
(葛城襲津彦) |founding year =4th century |cadet branches = }} The {{nihongo|'''Katsuragi clan'''|葛城氏|Katsuragi-uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group (''[[Uji (clan)|uji]]'') that supposedly existed during the [[Kofun period]]. [[Edmund Papinot|Papinot, Jacques Edmond Joseph]]. (1906). ''Dictionnaire d’histoire et de géographie du Japon''; Papinot, (2003). [http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5. ==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城襲津彦), a great-grandson of the legendary [[Emperor Kōgen]]. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the [[Ōtomo clan]] temporarily took its place. When [[Emperor Buretsu]] died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended [[Emperor Keitai]], a very distant imperial relative who resided in [[Koshi Province]], as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the [[Asuka period]].Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}. ==Family Tree== *[[Emperor Kōgen]] (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of [[Emperor Yūryaku]] *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******[[Princess Iwa|Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto]] (磐之媛命), empress of [[Emperor Nintoku]] ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of [[Emperor Richū]] *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of [[Emperor Richū]] ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼) ==See also== *[[Kofun period]] *[[Soga clan]] *[[Nakatomi clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] ==References== [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese clans]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katsuragi clan]][[ja:葛城氏]][[ko:가쓰라기씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620908]] Wikidata name: Katsuragi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11620908]] Wikidata name: Katsuragi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{refimprove|date=December 2017}}
{{family name hatnote|Katsuragi|lang=Japanese}}
{{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 葛城氏 |image = |image size = 140px |image caption = |home province = |parent house =[[File:Imperial Seal of Japan.svg|15px]] [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |founder =Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko |founding year =4th century |cadet branches = }}
The {{nihongo|'''Katsuragi clan'''|葛城氏|Katsuragi-uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group (''[[Uji (clan)|uji]]'') that flourished during the [[Kofun period]].<ref name="papinot2">[[:en:Edmund Papinot|Papinot, Jacques Edmond Joseph]]. (1906). ''Dictionnaire d’histoire et de géographie du Japon''; Papinot, (2003). [http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5.</ref>
==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城 襲津彦), a great-grandson of the legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] temporarily took its place. When [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]], a very distant imperial relative who resided in [[:en:Koshi Province (Japan)|Koshi Province]], as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].<ref>Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}.</ref>

The {{nihongo|'''Katsuragi clan'''|葛城氏|Katsuragi-uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group (''[[Uji (clan)|uji]]'') that flourished during the [[Kofun period]].<ref name="papinot2">[[:en:Edmund Papinot|Papinot, Jacques Edmond Joseph]]. (1906). ''Dictionnaire d’histoire et de géographie du Japon''; Papinot, (2003). [http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5.</ref>
==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城 襲津彦), a great-grandson of the legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] temporarily took its place. When [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]], a very distant imperial relative who resided in [[:en:Koshi Province (Japan)|Koshi Province]], as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].<ref>Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}.</ref>

However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].<ref>Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}.</ref>
==Family Tree== *[[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******[[:en:Princess Iwa|Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto]] (磐之媛命), empress of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi (title)|Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼)

The {{nihongo|'''Katsuragi clan'''|葛城氏|Katsuragi-uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group (''[[Uji (clan)|uji]]'') that flourished during the [[Kofun period]].<ref name="papinot2">[[:en:Edmund Papinot|Papinot, Jacques Edmond Joseph]]. (1906). ''Dictionnaire d’histoire et de géographie du Japon''; Papinot, (2003). [http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5.</ref>
==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城 襲津彦), a great-grandson of the legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] temporarily took its place. When [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]], a very distant imperial relative who resided in [[:en:Koshi Province (Japan)|Koshi Province]], as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].<ref>Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}.</ref>
==Family Tree== *[[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******[[:en:Princess Iwa|Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto]] (磐之媛命), empress of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi (title)|Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼)

==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城 襲津彦), a great-grandson of the legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] temporarily took its place. When [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]], a very distant imperial relative who resided in [[:en:Koshi Province (Japan)|Koshi Province]], as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].<ref>Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}.</ref>
==Family Tree== *[[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******[[:en:Princess Iwa|Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto]] (磐之媛命), empress of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi (title)|Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katsuragi clan]][[ja:葛城氏]][[ko:가쓰라기씨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katsuragi clan]][[ja:葛城氏]][[ko:가쓰라기씨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katsuragi clan]][[ja:葛城氏]][[ko:가쓰라기씨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katsuragi clan]][[ja:葛城氏]][[ko:가쓰라기씨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620908]] Wikidata name: Katsuragi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620908]] Wikidata name: Katsuragi clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{refimprove|date=December 2017}}
{{family name hatnote|Katsuragi|lang=Japanese}}
{{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 葛城氏 |image = |image size = 140px |image caption = |home province = |parent house =[[File:Imperial Seal of Japan.svg|15px]] [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |founder =Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko |founding year =4th century |cadet branches = }}
The {{nihongo|'''Katsuragi clan'''|葛城氏|Katsuragi-uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group (''[[Uji (clan)|uji]]'') that flourished during the [[Kofun period]].<ref name="papinot2">[[:en:Edmund Papinot|Papinot, Jacques Edmond Joseph]]. (1906). ''Dictionnaire d’histoire et de géographie du Japon''; Papinot, (2003). [http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5.</ref>
==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城 襲津彦), a great-grandson of the legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]] temporarily took its place. When [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]], a very distant imperial relative who resided in [[:en:Koshi Province (Japan)|Koshi Province]], as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].<ref>Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}.</ref>
==Family Tree== *[[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******[[:en:Princess Iwa|Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto]] (磐之媛命), empress of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi (title)|Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼)
==See also== *[[Kofun period]] *[[Soga clan]] *[[Nakatomi clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]]
==References== <references />

(1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}.</ref>

[http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5.</ref>
==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城 襲津彦), a great-grandson of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan}} temporarily took its place. When {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu}} died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}, a very distant imperial relative who resided in {{ill|Koshi Province (Japan)|en|Koshi Province (Japan)|WD=Q1351611|lt=Koshi Province}}, as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}.<ref>Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}.</ref>
==Family Tree== *{{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}} (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}} *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******{{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|WD=Q1066593|lt=Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto}} (磐之媛命), empress of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi (title)|Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū}} *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū}} ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼)

(1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}.</ref>

==Family Tree== *{{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}} (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******{{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|WD=Q1066593|lt=Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto|1=Princess Iwa}} (磐之媛命), empress of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi (title)|Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū|1=Emperor Richū}} *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū|1=Emperor Richū}} ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katsuragi clan]][[ja:葛城氏]][[ko:가쓰라기씨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katsuragi clan]][[ja:葛城氏]][[ko:가쓰라기씨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katsuragi clan]][[ja:葛城氏]][[ko:가쓰라기씨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Katsuragi clan]][[ja:葛城氏]][[ko:가쓰라기씨]]
{{refimprove|date=December 2017}}
{{family name hatnote|Katsuragi|lang=Japanese}}
{{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 葛城氏 |image = |image size = 140px |image caption = |home province = |parent house =[[File:Imperial Seal of Japan.svg|15px]] {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} |founder =Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko |founding year =4th century |cadet branches = }}
The {{nihongo|'''Katsuragi clan'''|葛城氏|Katsuragi-uji}} was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japanese|1=Japan}} aristocratic kin group (''[[Uji (clan)|uji]]'') that flourished during the [[Kofun period]].<ref name="papinot2">[[:en:Edmund Papinot|Papinot, Jacques Edmond Joseph]]. (1906). ''Dictionnaire d’histoire et de géographie du Japon''; Papinot, (2003). [http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf "Nakatomi," ''Nobiliare du Japon'', p. 39]; retrieved 2013-5-5.</ref>
==History== The Katsuragi clan was founded by Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko (葛城 襲津彦), a great-grandson of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}. During the 5th century the clan was the most prominent power in the court and intermarried with the imperial family. After the Katsuragi declined in the late 5th century, the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|qid=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan}} temporarily took its place. When {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|qid=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}} died with no apparent heir, it was [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] who recommended {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, a very distant imperial relative who resided in {{ill|Koshi Province (Japan)|en|Koshi Province (Japan)|qid=Q1351611|lt=Koshi Province|1=Koshi Province (Japan)}}, as the new monarch. However, Kanamura resigned due to the failure of his diplomatic policies, and the court was eventually controlled by the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] and [[Soga clan]]s at the beginning of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}.<ref>Brown, Delmer M. (1993). The Cambridge History of Japan: Ancient Japan. Cambridge University Press. p. 141. {{ISBN|0-521-22352-0}}.</ref>
==Family Tree== *{{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}} (孝元天皇, 273BC–158BC) **Hikofutsuoshinomakoto-no-mikoto (彦太忍信命, ?–?) ***Yanushioshiotakeogokoro-no-mikoto (屋主忍男武雄心命, ?–?) ****[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] (武内宿禰, ?–?) *****'''Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko''' (葛城襲津彦, ?–?) ******Katsuragi no Tamada no Sukune (葛城玉田宿禰, ?–416) *******Katsuragi no Tsubura no [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (葛城円大臣) ********Katsuragi no Karahime (葛城韓媛), wife of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|qid=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} *******Katsuragi no Kehime (葛城毛媛), wife of Kibi no Tasa (吉備田狭) ******{{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|qid=Q1066593|lt=Iwa-no-hime-no-mikoto|1=Princess Iwa}} (磐之媛命), empress of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} ******Katsuragi no Ashida no Sukune (葛城葦田宿禰) *******Katsuragi no Ari no [[Omi (title)|Omi]] (葛城蟻臣) ********Hae-hime (荑媛), wife of Ichinobe-no-Oshiwa-no-miko, crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|qid=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū|1=Emperor Richū}} *******Kuro-hime (黒媛), empress of {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|qid=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū|1=Emperor Richū}} ******Katsuragi no Koshimo no Sukune (葛城腰裾宿禰) ******Katsuragi no Ikuha no Toda no Sukune (的戸田宿禰) ******Kumamichi no Tarine (熊道足禰) ******name unknown *******name unknown ********Unakami no Sukune (菟上足尼)
